LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITIMORTGAGE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS TO EXPLAIN WHY PLAINTIFF DID NOT DISCOVER THE FACTS RELATED TO THE 2004 LOAN WHEN IT SEEMS APPARENT THAT NO WORK WAS BEING DONE ON THE ADDITIONAL BEDROOM. PLAINTIFF CANNOT IGNORE THE FACTS SET FORTH IN THE DOCUMENTS ATTACHED TO THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT RELATED TO THE RECEIPT OF THE NOTICE OF RIGHT TO CANCEL. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR WOO BEING DEFENDANT'S AGENT, NOT PLAINTIFF'S AGENT. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE HOW, WHEN, WHERE AND BY WHAT MEANS DEFENDANT COMMITTED CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Renotice Of Motion To Compel Deft Kenneth Woo To Respond To Discovery And For Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar May-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF VIRGINIA BALDOVIESO's Motion To Compel Defendant Kenneth Woo To Respond To Discovery And For Award Of Sanctions is denied. Responses had been served at the time the motion was filed, but there is no separate statement. Supplemental responses have been served since the motion was filed. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Defendant MEA Financial Services To Respond To Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Motion to compel defendant MEA Financial Services to respond to discovery; and for award of monetary sanctions granted:
1. All objections, except attorney-client and attorney work product privilege, are waived;
2. Defendant MEA Financial Services shall serve full, verified, and code compliant responses to plaintiff's specially prepared interrogatories, set one, within ten days of notice of entry of this order;
3. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $290.00, payable within thirty days of notice of entry of this order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITIMORTGAGE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Samantha N. Lamm, Esq. of Wolfe & Wyman LLP 949/475-9200 for deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall); no other appearances.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to explain why plaintiff did not discover the facts related to the 2004 loan when it seems apparent that no work was being done on the additional bedroom. Plaintiff cannot ignore the facts set forth in the documents attached to the original complaint related to the receipt of the notice of right to cancel. Plaintiff must plead facts that establish a basis for Woo being defendant's agent, not plaintiff's agent. Plaintiff must allege how, when, where and by what means defendant committed constructive fraud. Defendant to submit proposed form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
